message:
bd-zzss: Add non-interactive safety tests

- test_non_interactive_full_demo_completes: verifies piped output completes
- test_non_interactive_output_is_bounded: guards against unbounded output
- test_non_interactive_no_ansi_leakage: ensures no ANSI codes in no-color mode
- test_non_interactive_no_cursor_control: checks for dangerous cursor sequences
- test_non_interactive_live_auto_disabled: verifies no live updates when piped

All 34 e2e tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- One-liner: what the demo is
- Best command: `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full,tracing`

- Short bullet list of what it demonstrates

 {"id":"bd-2rqt","title":"API misuse detection tests","description":"## Problem
Users can easily misuse the API (e.g., using Cell::new() instead of Cell::from_markup()). We need tests that document these gotchas and ensure the API behaves predictably.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document all methods that accept strings but DON'T parse markup
- [ ] Document all methods that DO parse markup  
- [ ] Add tests that explicitly show the difference
- [ ] Consider adding deprecation warnings or lints for confusing APIs

## API Audit checklist
- [ ] Cell::new() - does NOT parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Cell::from_markup() - DOES parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Text::new() - does NOT parse markup
- [ ] Text::from_markup() - DOES parse markup
- [ ] Panel title/content - audit and document
- [ ] Tree labels - audit and document
- [ ] Progress descriptions - audit and document

## Files to modify
- Add doc comments to all relevant structs/methods
- tests/conformance/*.rs - add explicit documentation tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:09.661046714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T10:00:40.238099951Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T10:00:40.238082187Z","close_reason":"Documented markup parsing behavior across Text/Panel/Tree/Progress/Table; added conformance tests for Text vs markup","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
-{"id":"bd-2rxj","title":"demo_showcase: RichLogger integration","description":"Integrate RichLogger into demo_showcase and route logs through Live when active.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:42.813671250Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:34:18.411629130Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rxj","depends_on_id":"bd-1gou","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:10.277694461Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2rxj","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:12.822369090Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2rxj","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:42.825595399Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":45,"issue_id":"bd-2rxj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Integration

- Initialize `RichLogger` early in `main` when enabled by config.
- Use it to emit realistic logs during pipeline simulation (build/test/deploy).
- Avoid `println!` during Live; use console/logs so Live render hook can manage cursor control.

## Acceptance criteria

- Logs appear styled by level and include useful metadata (time/level/path where appropriate).
- Logger does not break non-interactive mode (still prints readable lines).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:13Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2sa2","title":"Style verification test helpers","description":"## Problem
Current tests often just check that output is non-empty or doesn't crash. We need test helpers that can actually VERIFY styles were applied correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create test_utils module with helper functions for style verification
- [ ] Helper to check if output contains ANSI color codes
- [ ] Helper to check if specific style was applied (bold, italic, color)
- [ ] Helper to extract styled segments from rendered output
- [ ] Document how to use these helpers in tests

## Implementation Notes
- Could check for ANSI escape sequences like \x1b[1m for bold
- Could provide segment inspection utilities
- Make it easy to write assertions like assert_has_bold!(output)

## Files to create/modify
- tests/test_utils.rs or tests/common/mod.rs - create helpers
- Update existing tests to use these helpers where appropriate","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:29.751175627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109472650Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109159270Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive style verification helpers to tests/common/assertions.rs: ansi module with constants, style assertions (bold/italic/underline/color), strip_ansi, and extract_styled_regions","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2sk1","title":"demo_showcase: automated test suite (unit + e2e + snapshots)","description":"Add comprehensive unit + integration + snapshot tests for demo_showcase (with detailed logging) to prevent regressions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:38.949498076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:51.444835092Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","qa","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2sk1","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:38.968252493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":65,"issue_id":"bd-2sk1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why this exists

The showcase is meant to be a *flagship* artifact, so it needs flagship testing:
- Output regressions are subtle and easy to introduce during refactors.
- Live/TTY behavior can break silently without automated checks.
- Export artifacts (HTML/SVG) are particularly prone to drift.

## Testing philosophy

We want three layers:

1. **Unit tests (fast, internal)**
   - CLI parsing, config defaults, scene registry ordering
   - small pure helpers (badges/formatting)

2. **Integration/e2e smoke tests (black-box)**
   - Spawn the compiled binary via `CARGO_BIN_EXE_demo_showcase`
   - Run each scene with CI-safe flags (`--no-live --no-screen --no-interactive --quick`)
   - Assert exit code, bounded runtime (timeout), and key output markers

3. **Snapshot/golden tests (visual regression)**
   - Capture deterministic text output (fixed `--width/--height`, `--color-system none` or ANSI-stripped)
   - Use `insta` snapshots for key scenes

## Logging requirements

All integration tests should emit structured debug logs (via `test-log` + `tracing`) including:
- command line executed
- env overrides
- runtime duration
- output sizes
- first/last N lines on failure (without spamming CI)

## Acceptance criteria

- `cargo test` passes quickly and runs unit + basic smoke tests.
- `cargo test --features full,tracing` runs the full snapshot suite (including content scenes).
- E2E tests fail with actionable logs when a scene hangs or output changes unexpectedly.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2smc","title":"demo_showcase: dashboard layout + live updates","description":"Build the core split-screen dashboard (Layout) and drive it with Live updates (progress/logs).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:36.694609635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:38:49.077869634Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","layout","live"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-1388","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:38:49.077815984Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:30.240263939Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:27.173009186Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:36.713272217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-2smc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Dashboard concept

Create a single “mission control” dashboard that stays on screen and updates live:
- **Header**: app name + run id + clock + key status badges
- **Main**: pipeline progress (multi-step), current action panel
- **Sidebar**: service status table + quick facts
- **Footer**: streaming logs (tail) + hints (“press q”, etc.)

This should be the primary wow-moment: layout + live refresh + dense styling.

## Technical approach

- Model a `DemoState` struct (progress values, current step, log ring buffer, service status, etc.).
- Use `Live::get_renderable(|| Box<dyn Renderable>)` with a closure that builds a fresh `Layout` from the current state snapshot.
- Default to `LiveOptions { screen: true, ... }` when interactive; provide flags to disable.
- Keep refresh rate modest (e.g. 10 fps max) and driven by `LiveOptions.refresh_per_second`.

## Acceptance criteria

- In an interactive terminal, the dashboard updates smoothly and exits cleanly (cursor restored; no leftover alt-screen unless `--screen`).
- In non-interactive mode, a static “snapshot” dashboard is printed once (no live loop).
- Layout adapts to narrow terminals: if width < threshold, collapse sidebar or switch to single-column mode.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:46Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-6ozb","title":"demo_showcase: final polish + QA","description":"Polish pass: visual tweaks, timing, edge cases; ensure all quality gates pass and demo feels ‘product grade’.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:28.642592479Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:17.783744643Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","polish","qa"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-164e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:15.925273437Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-2175","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:08.824416694Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:35.835459296Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-2smc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:06.360421270Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:13.617768058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3bid","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:18.150875345Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:28.660699129Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3rhg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:22.625349203Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3vf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:11.348492415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-ohsq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:20.376258928Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Checklist

- Run full quality gates:
  - `cargo fmt --check`
  - `cargo check --all-targets`
  - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - `cargo test`
- Visual pass on at least:
  - wide terminal (>= 120 cols)
  - narrow terminal (~70 cols)
  - piped output (`| cat`)
- Ensure the demo ends cleanly: cursor restored, alt-screen disabled, no stray control codes.

## Acceptance criteria

- Demo looks “designed”, not like a pile of examples.
- All scenes have consistent titles, spacing, and style usage.
- Total runtime feels appropriate; `--quick` exists for CI/dev.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:37Z"},{"id":77,"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Update: automated regression suite is required

Final QA must include the automated demo_showcase regression suite (`bd-2sk1`):
- per-scene smoke tests
- snapshot tests
- export bundle e2e tests
- non-interactive safety tests

This reduces reliance on manual eyeballing and prevents “works on my terminal” regressions.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6tj5","title":"demo_showcase tests: CLI + Config unit tests","description":"Expand unit tests for CLI/config: defaults, invalid flags, and new flags like --seed/--links.","notes":"Added 12 new unit tests covering: default config values, --quick, --force-terminal, -h short form, all color system variants, missing flag values, non-finite/negative speed, all boolean pairs, width/height non-integer, positional arg rejection, and combined flags.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:04.773473520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:46:48.320559684Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:46:48.320428780Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6tj5","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:04.794750840Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6tj5","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:16.075340749Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"bd-6tj5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit test scope

Expand unit tests inside `src/bin/demo_showcase.rs` to cover:
- default config values (including derived defaults like interactive/live/screen auto modes)
- parsing of numeric flags (`--speed`, `--width`, `--height`, `--seed`)
- boolean flag pairs (`--foo` / `--no-foo`)
- mutual exclusion rules (e.g. `--export` vs `--export-dir`)
- friendly error messages

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests are deterministic and do not require a TTY.
- Test failures point directly to the CLI regression.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-86gp","title":"demo_showcase: capture output for export","description":"Add capture mode to demo runner so scenes can be captured to segments for exporting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownIsland","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:53.628166487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:31:34.609990369Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:31:34.609928834Z","close_reason":"Export capture implemented: run_export() captures console output and writes HTML/SVG files. All 73 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-86gp","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:41.570665666Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-86gp","depends_on_id":"bd-164e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:53.642928274Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-86gp","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:39.207097640Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":50,"issue_id":"bd-86gp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Approach

- When `--export` or `--export-dir` is set, run the demo with capture enabled:
  - `console.begin_capture()`
  - run selected scenes
  - then call `export_html` / `export_svg`

Be careful: capture + Live/alt-screen don't mix well. For export runs, prefer non-live mode (static output).

## Acceptance criteria

- Export run produces stable artifacts without requiring a TTY.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:06Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-cisk","title":"demo_showcase snapshots: debug tools","description":"Add insta snapshot coverage for debug_tools output (pretty/inspect + traceback).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:26.212372214Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:19.830717662Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","snapshots","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cisk","depends_on_id":"bd-11zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:12.481243908Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-cisk","depends_on_id":"bd-1365","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:19.830681455Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-cisk","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:17.433057178Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-cisk","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:14.925073557Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-cisk","depends_on_id":"bd-yzr8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:26.227535266Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":86,"issue_id":"bd-cisk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Snapshot contract

Snapshot `debug_tools` scene output (pretty/inspect + traceback) in no-color or ANSI-stripped mode.

Ensure traceback and inspect outputs are stable for a fixed seed/config.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-eb17","title":"Port rich.prompt/status/pager equivalents (interactive helpers)","description":"Implement the interactive helper layer from Python Rich.

Scope (Rust-idiomatic):
- Prompt: styled input with validation and defaults
- Status: spinner + message context manager
- Pager: send output through pager when available

Acceptance criteria:
- Cross-platform behavior (Unix + Windows) behind crossterm
- Unit tests for parsing/validation logic; integration tests for non-interactive fallback

 {"id":"bd-fqh6","title":"interactive: fix test_prompt_from_reader_validates_and_reprompts failure","description":"Pre-existing test failure in interactive::tests. The test expects validation error message 'digits only' to be printed to the buffer but the assertion fails. Needs investigation.","notes":"Fixed: test assertion was checking for literal 'digits only\n' but ANSI style codes appear between text and newline.  {"id":"bd-yzr8","title":"demo_showcase tests: snapshot suite (golden output)","description":"Add `insta` snapshot tests for key scenes using deterministic flags (fixed width/height, ANSI stripped).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:09.899765545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:12.046811876Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","snapshots","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-11zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:43.998207824Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-1365","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:53.291720395Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:48.662582585Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:09.915171991Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:50.940799339Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:56.041645930Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-5bjf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:46.300688024Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":69,"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Snapshot strategy

- Prefer snapshotting ANSI-stripped output to avoid terminal-dependent differences.
- Use fixed dimensions via `--width/--height` and deterministic seed via `--seed`.
- Snapshot only the most important scenes (hero, dashboard snapshot mode, debug_tools) to keep snapshot maintenance manageable.

## Acceptance criteria

- A meaningful visual regression suite exists (not just trivial snapshots).
- Snapshots are stable across machines/CI.

- Running in pipes/non-TTY must never hang.
- Live/alt-screen should be auto-disabled unless forced.
- Output must be bounded (no infinite animation frames).

- Run the full demo (or a representative subset) in CI-safe mode with a hard timeout.
- Assert output length is within a sane upper bound (guard against runaway loops).

## Acceptance criteria

- A regression that would cause `... | cat` to hang fails the test with clear logs.

 {"id":"rich_rust-01n","title":"Validate Text Wrapping against RICH_SPEC Section 13","description":"## Objective
Verify the text wrapping implementation matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 13 exactly.

## Background
Text wrapping is critical for responsive terminal output. The algorithm must handle word boundaries, long words, wide characters, and styled text spans correctly.

## Validation Checklist

### 13.1 Word Boundary Detection
- [ ] Verify words split on whitespace
- [ ] Verify hyphenated words treated as single unit (or split at hyphen)
- [ ] Verify CJK characters can wrap between any characters

### 13.2 Wrap Algorithm
- [ ] Verify words fit: add to current line
- [ ] Verify word too long: break at width (fold mode)
- [ ] Verify line break: start new line, preserve indent
- [ ] Verify whitespace at line break: consumed (not carried)

### 13.3 Overflow Methods
- [ ] Verify Fold: break anywhere when word > width
- [ ] Verify Crop: truncate at width
- [ ] Verify Ellipsis: truncate at width-1 and add ellipsis

### 13.4 Style Preservation
- [ ] Verify spans are split correctly across line breaks
- [ ] Verify style continues on wrapped lines
- [ ] Verify partial spans at wrap point

### 13.5 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test wrapping with wide characters (2-cell)
- [ ] Test wrapping with zero-width characters
- [ ] Test wrapping at exactly width boundary
- [ ] Test no_wrap flag
- [ ] Test empty text
- [ ] Test single long word

## Files to Examine
- src/text.rs (wrap methods)
- src/cells.rs (width calculation)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 13","acceptance_criteria":"All wrap scenarios work correctly. Styles preserved across line breaks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:19:12.644260470Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T04:44:58.001495493Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T04:44:58.001495493Z","close_reason":"Validated Text Wrapping against RICH_SPEC Section 13. All 41 E2E tests and 17 unit tests pass. Implementation correctly handles: word boundary detection via character iteration, wrap algorithm with proper line division, all 4 overflow methods (Fold, Crop, Ellipsis, Ignore), style preservation through slice()/divide(), and edge cases (empty text, whitespace, zero width, wide chars). No gaps found.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-01n","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:35:49Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-02i","title":"Verify Markup Parser Error Recovery","description":"## Objective
Ensure markup parser gracefully handles malformed input as specified in RICH_SPEC.md Section 4.

## Background
Users will make mistakes in markup. The parser should render as much as possible and treat invalid markup as literal text rather than crashing.

## Technical Details

### Error Recovery Cases

#### Unclosed Tags
Input: '[bold]hello'
Expected: render 'hello' with bold, auto-close at end

#### Unmatched Close Tags
Input: 'hello[/bold]'
Expected: render '[/bold]' as literal text

#### Invalid Style in Tag
Input: '[invalidstyle]hello[/]'
Expected: render '[invalidstyle]hello[/]' as literal OR apply null style

#### Nested Mismatches
Input: '[bold][italic]hello[/bold][/italic]'
Expected: reasonable behavior (spec should define)

#### Empty Tags
Input: '[]hello'
Expected: render '[]hello' as literal OR ignore empty tag

#### Escaped Brackets
Input: 'price is \[100\]'
Expected: render 'price is [100]'

### Verification Steps
1. Write test for each error case
2. Compare to Python Rich behavior
3. Document any intentional deviations
4. Ensure no panics/crashes on any input

## Files to Examine
- src/markup/mod.rs (parser)
- Python Rich markup.py (reference)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 4.4","acceptance_criteria":"Parser handles all malformed input gracefully. No panics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:22:31.584465299Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T05:00:48.345989669Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T05:00:48.345989669Z","close_reason":"VERIFICATION COMPLETE: Markup parser error recovery fully implemented and tested.

Verified against RICH_SPEC.md Section 4:

1. **Unclosed Tags** (`[bold]hello`):
   - Lines 229-236: Auto-closes at end ✓
   - Test: test_render_unclosed_tag ✓
   - MATCHES SPEC Section 4.5

2. **Unmatched Close Tags** (`hello[/bold]`):
   - Lines 199-214: Returns MarkupError::UnmatchedClosingTag ✓
   - Test: test_unmatched_closing_tag ✓
   - MATCHES SPEC Section 4.6

3. **Implicit Close with Empty Stack** (`hello[/]`):
   - Line 207: Returns error ✓
   - Test: test_empty_close_nothing_to_close ✓

4. **Invalid Style in Tag** (`[invalidstyle]hello[/]`):
   - Line 268: Falls back to Style::new() gracefully ✓
   - Test: test_invalid_style_graceful ✓

5. **Empty Tags** (`[]hello`):
   - Regex requires [a-z#/@...], [] doesn't match ✓
   - Test: test_empty_tag_content ✓

6. **Escaped Brackets** (`\[100]`):
   - Lines 108, 122-128, 192-194 ✓
   - Tests: test_render_escaped_bracket, test_mixed_escaped_and_tags ✓
   - MATCHES SPEC Section 4.4

7. **Nested Mismatches**:
   - pop_matching() searches stack for explicit closes ✓
   - Test: test_nested_with_explicit_close ✓

8. **Graceful Fallback** (render_or_plain):
   - Lines 282-284: Returns plain text on error ✓
   - Test: test_render_or_plain_fallback ✓

9. **No Panic Tests**:
   - test_no_panic_on_random_brackets covers edge cases ✓
   - Unicode/emoji tests pass ✓

All 15 markup tests pass. Implementation is spec-compliant.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-02i","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:35:49Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-02i","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-qgs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:35:49Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-0sw","title":"Validate Table Rendering against RICH_SPEC Section 9.5-9.6","description":"## Objective
Verify Table rendering (row rendering, border drawing) matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.5-9.6.

## Background
This covers the visual output of tables: how rows are rendered, how borders are drawn, header/footer handling.

## Validation Checklist

### 9.5 Row Rendering
- [ ] render_row() produces correct segments
- [ ] Cell content is wrapped to column width
- [ ] Vertical alignment (top/middle/bottom) applied correctly
- [ ] Padding applied inside cells
- [ ] Multi-line cells: all lines padded to same width
- [ ] Border characters inserted between columns
- [ ] Row style applied to cell backgrounds

### 9.6 Full Table Rendering
- [ ] Title rendered above table (centered by default)
- [ ] Header row uses header_style
- [ ] Header row separator uses head_row from BoxChars
- [ ] Body rows alternate row_styles if provided
- [ ] Row separators (when show_lines=true) use row from BoxChars
- [ ] Footer row uses footer from BoxChars
- [ ] Caption rendered below table
- [ ] Edge borders controlled by show_edge

### Box Character Usage
- [ ] Top border: box.top (only if show_edge)
- [ ] Header separator: box.head_row
- [ ] Row separator: box.row (only if show_lines)
- [ ] Footer separator: box.foot_row (if footer present)
- [ ] Bottom border: box.bottom (only if show_edge)
- [ ] Column dividers: box.vertical

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty table (no rows)
- [ ] No header (show_header=false)
- [ ] No edge borders (show_edge=false)
- [ ] No row lines (show_lines=false)
- [ ] Single column table
- [ ] Single row table

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (rendering functions)
- src/box.rs (BoxChars)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.5-9.6","acceptance_criteria":"Table renders correctly with all combinations of options.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:46:42.754066074Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T18:21:01.430656253Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T18:21:01.430656253Z","close_reason":"## Validation Complete - Table Rendering

### Summary
Table rendering implementation is **MOSTLY ALIGNED** with RICH_SPEC.md Sections 9.5-9.6. The main limitation is **single-line cell rendering only** - multi-line cells with vertical alignment are not supported.

---

### 9.5 Full Table Rendering ✅ ALIGNED

**render() function (lines 641-738)** follows spec structure:

| Step | Spec | Impl | Status |
|------|------|------|--------|
| Calculate widths | calculate_column_widths() | calculate_widths() | ✅ |
| Render title | if self.title | if let Some(title) | ✅ |
| Top border | if show_edge | if self.show_edge | ✅ |
| Header | if show_header | if self.show_header | ✅ |
| Header separator | head_sep | HeadRow level | ✅ |
| Data rows | iterate rows | iterate self.rows | ✅ |
| Row styles | alternating row_styles | row_styles mod | ✅ |
| Row separators | if show_lines/end_section | if show_lines/end_section | ✅ |
| Footer | if show_footer | if self.show_footer | ✅ |
| Footer separator | foot_sep | FootRow level | ✅ |
| Bottom border | if show_edge | if self.show_edge | ✅ |
| Caption | if self.caption | if let Some(caption) | ✅ |

---

### 9.6 Row Rendering ⚠️ SIMPLIFIED

**render_row_content() (lines 797-890)** is simplified vs spec's render_row():

| Feature | Spec | Impl | Status |
|---------|------|------|--------|
| Single-line cells | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Multi-line cells | Lines array | Not supported | ⚠️ MISSING |
| Vertical alignment | align_top/middle/bottom | Not implemented | ⚠️ MISSING |
| Content wrapping | column width | Truncates instead | ⚠️ DIFFERS |
| Horizontal padding | pad_str | pad_str | ✅ |
| Cell dividers | box_style.head[2] | box_chars.head[2] | ✅ |
| Left/right edges | show_edge | self.show_edge | ✅ |
| Style combination | row_style + cell_style | combined_style | ✅ |

**Impact**: Tables with long content are truncated, not wrapped. Cells cannot span multiple lines.

---

### Box Character Usage ✅ CORRECT

| Border Type | Spec RowLevel | Impl RowLevel | Status |
|-------------|---------------|---------------|--------|
| Top border | Top | Top | ✅ |
| Header sep | Head | HeadRow | ✅ |
| Row sep | Row | Row | ✅ |
| Footer sep | Foot | FootRow | ✅ |
| Bottom border | Bottom | Bottom | ✅ |
| Cell dividers | head[2] | head[2] | ✅ |

BoxChars struct (src/box.rs) has all required character arrays:
- ASCII, SQUARE, ROUNDED, DOUBLE box styles ✅
- get_row_chars() method ✅
- build_row() method ✅

---

### Edge Cases ✅ TESTED

E2E tests cover:
- Empty table (e2e_table_empty) ✅
- Single column (e2e_table_single_column) ✅
- Single cell (e2e_table_single_cell) ✅
- Empty cells (e2e_table_empty_cells) ✅
- No header (show_header=false) ✅
- No edges (show_edge=false) ✅
- Row lines (show_lines=true) ✅
- Footer (show_footer=true) ✅

---

### Overall Assessment: ✅ PASS (with simplifications)

**Correct**:
- Full table rendering structure matches spec
- Box character usage correct
- Edge cases handled
- All configuration options work

**Simplified/Missing**:
1. **Multi-line cells**: Not supported (content truncated)
2. **Vertical alignment**: Column.vertical field exists but not used
3. **Text wrapping**: Content exceeding width is truncated, not wrapped

These simplifications are acceptable for Phase 1 but should be tracked for future enhancement.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-1","validation"]}

@@ -293,3 +293,152 @@ fn test_width_override() {
     // With narrow width, output should wrap or be narrower
     // Just verify it runs successfully
 }
+
+// ============================================================================
+// Non-interactive safety tests (bd-zzss)
+// ============================================================================
+
+/// Guards against regressions that would cause piped output to hang.
+/// Uses deterministic, bounded settings: --quick --seed 0 --color-system none
+#[test]
+fn test_non_interactive_full_demo_completes() {
+    common::init_test_logging();
+
+    let result = DemoRunner::new()
+        .arg("--quick")
+        .arg("--seed")
+        .arg("0")
+        .arg("--color-system")
+        .arg("none")
+        .arg("--no-interactive")
+        .timeout_secs(30) // Hard timeout - must complete in 30s
+        .run()
+        .expect("should run");
+
+    assert_success(&result);
+    assert_no_timeout(&result);
+
+    // Should complete all scenes
+    assert_stdout_contains(&result, "scenes completed");
+}
+
+/// Guards against unbounded output (infinite animation frames, etc.)
+#[test]
+fn test_non_interactive_output_is_bounded() {
+    common::init_test_logging();
+
+    let result = DemoRunner::new()
+        .arg("--quick")
+        .arg("--seed")
+        .arg("0")
+        .arg("--color-system")
+        .arg("none")
+        .arg("--no-interactive")
+        .timeout_secs(30)
+        .run()
+        .expect("should run");
+
+    assert_success(&result);
+
+    // Output should be reasonably bounded
+    // A full demo run should produce less than 100KB of output
+    // This guards against runaway loops that spam infinite frames
+    let output_size = result.stdout.len() + result.stderr.len();
+    const MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES: usize = 100 * 1024; // 100 KB
+    assert!(
+        output_size < MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES,
+        "Output size ({} bytes) exceeds limit ({} bytes) - possible unbounded output",
+        output_size,
+        MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES
+    );
+}
+
+/// Verifies no ANSI control sequences leak when color is disabled.
+#[test]
+fn test_non_interactive_no_ansi_leakage() {
+    common::init_test_logging();
+
+    let result = DemoRunner::new()
+        .arg("--quick")
+        .arg("--seed")
+        .arg("0")
+        .arg("--color-system")
+        .arg("none")
+        .arg("--no-interactive")
+        .no_color()
+        .timeout_secs(30)
+        .run()
+        .expect("should run");
+
+    assert_success(&result);
+
+    // No ANSI escape sequences should appear in output
+    assert!(
+        !result.stdout.contains("\x1b["),
+        "Stdout should not contain ANSI escape codes in no-color mode"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        !result.stderr.contains("\x1b["),
+        "Stderr should not contain ANSI escape codes in no-color mode"
+    );
+}
+
+/// Verifies no cursor control sequences that could cause display issues.
+#[test]
+fn test_non_interactive_no_cursor_control() {
+    common::init_test_logging();
+
+    let result = DemoRunner::new()
+        .arg("--quick")
+        .arg("--seed")
+        .arg("0")
+        .arg("--color-system")
+        .arg("none")
+        .arg("--no-interactive")
+        .timeout_secs(30)
+        .run()
+        .expect("should run");
+
+    assert_success(&result);
+
+    // Should not contain cursor movement sequences
+    // \x1b[H = cursor home, \x1b[?25l = hide cursor, \x1b[2J = clear screen
+    let dangerous_sequences = ["\x1b[H", "\x1b[?25", "\x1b[2J", "\x1b[?1049"];
+    for seq in dangerous_sequences {
+        assert!(
+            !result.stdout.contains(seq),
+            "Stdout should not contain cursor control sequence '{}'",
+            seq.escape_default()
+        );
+    }
+}
+
+/// Tests that live mode is auto-disabled in non-interactive context.
+#[test]
+fn test_non_interactive_live_auto_disabled() {
+    common::init_test_logging();
+
+    // Even with --live flag, non-TTY should auto-disable live mode
+    // This is harder to test directly, but we can verify output is static
+    let result = DemoRunner::new()
+        .arg("--quick")
+        .arg("--seed")
+        .arg("0")
+        .arg("--no-interactive")
+        .no_color()
+        .timeout_secs(30)
+        .run()
+        .expect("should run");
+
+    assert_success(&result);
+    assert_no_timeout(&result);
+
+    // Output should be static (no carriage returns for live updates)
+    // Newlines are fine, but \r without \n indicates live updates
+    let cr_without_lf = result.stdout.matches('\r').count() - result.stdout.matches("\r\n").count();
+    assert!(
+        cr_without_lf == 0,
+        "Found {} carriage returns without line feeds - indicates live updates in non-interactive mode",
+        cr_without_lf
+    );
+}
